YTxp7AG1zLc.text: two years ago, the bbc built [music] a robot crab to spy on a real crab colony and it successfully blended into the crowd. but during the observation, a stingray attacked the group and started [music] eating them. the crabs know a stingray can devour 50 of them a day, but only the ones with soft shells. so, they have a rule. the hard-shelled crabs protect [music] the soft ones and hide them at the very bottom of the pile. but the robot couldn't hide and the stingray damaged it. when the crabs saw this, they actually crawled over to save their buddy and covered it with their hard shells. the stingray couldn't attack it anymore and swam away hungry. in the end, the crabs risked their lives for [music] a piece of plastic. it turns out that solidarity exists even in the animal kingdom, even if it's not a robot.

NFaU5qGN9MU.text: almost a decade ago, the bbc [music] built a hyper-realistic robot baby langur monkey to spy on a real monkey troop. but during the observation, the mechanical baby accidentally fell from a high tree branch and lay [music] completely still on the ground. the monkeys know how fragile their babies are, so they genuinely believed the newborn had died. [music] because of this, a heavy silence fell over the entire jungle as the troop gathered around the motionless body. but the robot couldn't wake up, and the monkeys [music] began to mourn. when the troop saw this, they actually hugged each other for comfort, touched the fake baby gently, and held hands in collective grief. [music] in the end, these animals showed deeper empathy for a robot than most humans show for each other.

Md9UL7iHvPc.text: how many spider elephants would it take to get onto the leaderboard and steal a brain rock? the spider elephant is a very exclusive brain rock that costs 6 million robux to buy the spider chain ugc, but in return, you get this non-tradable, non-stealable brain rock. so, how many spider elephants would it actually take to hit the leaderboard? well, if you divide the top player's 13.19 trillion per second by the spider elephant's generation, it would take 13,190 spider elephants to land number one on the leaderboard. but, the elephant actually comes with the spider trait naturally, bumping its generation up to 4.5 billion a second. if we redo the math, it would only take around 2,131 spider elephants to steal the number one spot. sadly, none of this is possible because only 25 spider elephants exist.
